update: You can now update the encryption key of your encrypted live stream contents while the channel is running.
You can now  create AI-generated captions and translated captions  for a live stream.
You can now preview your input streams with ultra-low latency, which allows you to take corrective actions and maintain high-quality viewing experience for your viewers.
You can now  distribute live stream content to remote endpoints  by using the Secure Reliable Transport (SRT) protocol or the Real-Time Messaging Protocol (RTMP).
You can now generate Web Video Text Tracks format (WebVTT) subtitles from cea608 or cea708 embedded captions in the input stream.
Added support for  H.265 (HEVC) inputs and outputs , which allows for more efficient compression.
Added support for UHD (4K) inputs and outputs.
You can now  create a DVR session  for a past, current, or future live stream.
The Live Stream API is now available in  asia-south1  and  europe-north1 . For more information, see  Live Stream API locations .
You can now  create static overlays  and position them on top of a live stream.
You can now  create video on demand (VOD) clips from a live stream .

Slates  are now supported. Slates are video or image content that can replace the main live stream content whenever you want (for example, if you are experiencing technical issues on the input stream).
You can now use VPC Service Controls to  secure your live streams .
Added the  timecode feature  which supports synchronizing media workflows with live stream content
Added new channel events:  mute ,  unmute ,  return to program , and  switch input
Added  audio loudness normalization  and  audio gain control
Pricing  is now regional. See  Locations  for available regions.
Live Stream API  is now in GA.
